checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully extracts text present in the source document. No fabricated body text, sections, or headings were identified. The metadata and section titles are derived directly from the document's header content.

Every name, title, and phone number in the output matches the source PDF exactly. 
Program Contact: Vicki Vitiello, 919/814-6504.
Financial Contact: Burdette Southerland, 919/814-6510.

The document is a single page, and the primary page content (Authorization, Contacts, Description, Funding status) is present in the output. While some minor text elements from the margins are missing, no entire sections or pages were omitted.
The source document does not contain any structured tables; funding information is presented as text/form fields which have been captured in paragraphs.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Three distinct lines of text from the source are missing in the output: the LGC review stamp, the document date, and the footer form identifier. Together, these represent approximately 10-12 words (~8% of the document content). This does not meet the 25% threshold for a MAJOR defect under this check.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture several discrete information points present in the source: the document date ('APRIL 2020'), the administrative review status ('LGC Reviewed in accordance to G.S. 159-34(a)'), and the form identifier footer ('E Short Form 148 1'). These omissions constitute a loss of specific metadata and compliance information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Top-left stamp: 'LGC Reviewed in accordance to G.S. 159-34(a)'; Top-right: 'APRIL 2020'; Footer: 'E Short Form 148 1'
checks.b_evidence_output: (Text is completely missing from elements and metadata fields)

checks.a_analysis: The output captures each content block from the source document (program list title, authorization, agency details, contact information, program description, and funding source) exactly once. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or repeated sections.

The hierarchy is logically structured. The metadata title and section title represent the primary subject (H1). The agency name is set as H2, and sub-categories like 'Agency Contact Person' and 'Brief Description of Program' are set as H3. This is a consistent and logical progression (H1 -> H2 -> H3).

The source document does not contain formal data tables. The contact information is presented in columns in the source but is correctly serialized as headings and paragraphs in the output, which is an acceptable representation of side-by-side textual content.

The output follows the logical flow of the source document from top to bottom: Title -> State Authorization -> Agency Name -> Contact Info -> Description -> Funding details. The side-by-side contact boxes are read sequentially (Program then Financial), which is the standard reading order for such layouts.

The document contains a list of programs in the title area and checkbox items at the bottom. These have been appropriately handled as a heading and paragraph strings respectively, maintaining the meaning and relationship of the items.
There are no web links present in the source document, and none have been erroneously injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization is significantly compromised in the contact information section. While the transition to a single-column layout is acceptable, the rendering has failed for the financial contact's phone number. The string 'Number 919/814-6510' has been detached from its label 'Phone' and is rendered outside the main content column, floating in the margin to the left of the 'Brief Description of Program:' heading. This breaks the visual flow and logic of the document.
checks.b_evidence_output: Number 919/814-6510 (rendered in the left margin, detached from the heading and main content area)

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain traditional bordered tables. The multi-column layout for contact information is correctly flattened (though with the layout bug noted in V1), and the checklist-style sections for 'Organizations Funded' and 'Source of Funds' are rendered clearly as text with brackets and underscores.

The source PDF contains an official 'LGC Reviewed' stamp logo in the top-left corner (in accordance to G.S. 159-34(a)). This is a meaningful indicator of document status and certification. This stamp/logo is entirely missing from the HTML output header.
checks.b_evidence_source: LGC Reviewed in accordance to G.S. 159-34(a) stamp/logo at the top-left of page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The logo is absent from the top of the HTML page.

checks.a_analysis: There is no visual duplication of body content. While headings appear in both the Table of Contents sidebar and the main body, this is an intentional and acceptable design choice for accessibility. No paragraphs or data blocks are repeated incorrectly.
